← all tutorials

Chain Replication — interactive 📖 CHAIN_REPLICATION.md 📄 chain_replication.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
Replicas line up in a chain: the HEAD takes all writes (forwarding them down), the TAIL takes all reads. A write is "complete" only at the tail, so reads always return the latest fully-replicated value — strong consistency with no per-op consensus. Click a node to crash it and watch the head/tail reassign with zero data loss. The default 4-node write path matches chain_replication.py Section A / GOLD CHECK.

① The chain — write flows head→tail, read hits the tail

What to do: press Send write to watch the value travel left→right down the chain (3 hops for N=4). It is only "complete" once it reaches the TAIL. Then press Send read — the client asks the TAIL, which holds every completed write. Each node shows its latest applied value.
N = 4, write latency = N−1 = 3 hops
HEAD (writes) TAIL (reads) interior (relay) value in flight
In words: writes serialize along ONE path, so every replica applies them in the SAME order (FIFO). Reads hit only the tail, whose history is a prefix of all completed writes — so a read never sees a partial write. No quorum counted.

② Failure handling — click a node to crash it, head/tail reassign

What this shows: a separate configuration manager (e.g. ZooKeeper) removes crashed nodes and reassigns the ends. Crash the HEAD → its successor becomes head (it already has all writes, since they flowed through it). Crash the TAIL → its predecessor becomes tail (writes flowed through it before the tail). Try it, then write/read on the short chain.
click any live node to crash / revive it
HEAD TAIL crashed
Unlike Raft (needs a majority of N), the chain keeps serving down to a single node — it becomes both head and tail. Correctness still depends on the manager detecting failures.

③ Chain vs Raft — O(N) latency vs O(N) leader work

What this shows: same total messages, opposite shapes. Chain serializes the write (N−1 sequential hops, each node O(1) work, no consensus). Raft fans out in parallel (1 round, but the leader does O(N) work and counts a majority). Drag N to see chain's latency climb while Raft's stays flat.
1 update; 1 hop / round = 1 unit
Read the dial: low latency + beefy leader → Raft. Spread load + tolerate serial latency → chain. Chain pushes consensus off the critical path — only the manager runs it.

④ Gold check — the full pipeline, recomputed live in JS

What this proves: the page re-runs the whole pipeline in your browser — writes/reads on a 4-node chain, then crash the head and keep writing, then crash the tail and keep reading — on the identical operations as chain_replication.py GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms every read returns the latest fully-replicated write, with zero data loss across both failovers.
check: … JS pipeline matches chain_replication.py GOLD CHECK (reads=[1,2,3,4,4,5], final_read=5, no data loss).
